title: symphyotrichum spathulatum var. intermedium
intro: Symphyotrichum spathulatum var. intermedium, also known as the western yarrow or intermediate western yarrow, is a perennial plant native to western North America, characterized by its fern-like foliage and white or pinkish flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, which have been used to treat various health issues such as wounds, fever, and digestive problems. The plant is rich in flavonoids, sesquiterpenes, and other bioactive compounds, which have been shown to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects, contributing to its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. The plant can be used in various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
